channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard’s exit weakens MAGA’s anti-war faction
item.description: Her resignation shows how little influence such voices wield around Donald Trump

item.title: Trump’s About-Face on U.S. Troops in Poland Mystifies Allies
item.description: The abrupt policy reversal leaves NATO allies wondering to what extent the United States will defend Europe.

item.title: What the U.S. Should Have Learned From Past Ebola Outbreaks
item.description: The West’s shortsightedness in Africa is more apparent than ever.

item.title: ‘The Boys’ Turned Superhero Satire Against American Politics
item.description: The series has been the most on-the-nose fictional take on Trumpism so far.

item.title: The Book About the U.S. Military That Everyone Should Be Reading Now
item.description: Kori Schake’s ‘The State and the Soldier’ is the discussion on civilian control of the military that this moment requires.

item.title: ‘Atropia’ Takes on the War on Terror
item.description: For a movie set 20 years ago, it has a surprising amount to say about today’s wars in the Middle East.

item.title: The Next Pandemic Will Come From a Conflict Zone
item.description: The latest Ebola outbreak underscores a central flaw with the global preparedness model.

item.title: Xi’s Flurry of Post-Trump Diplomacy
item.description: The Chinese president may be preparing for a rare and momentous trip to Pyongyang.

item.title: How Kazakhstan’s Super-Apps Outpace the Law
item.description: The fundamental difference between Kazakhstan’s fintech landscape and Western financial systems lies not in the "right to block," but in what happens after a service is suspended.

item.title: Reweaving the Ruins of the American Secret War
item.description: Peace is not given – it is forged, reclaimed, and built from the very fragments meant to destroy us.

item.title: You probably don’t need extra electrolytes
item.description: Unless you’re athletic or unwell

item.title: South Korea’s Nuclear Submarine Push Is a Test of Non-nuclear Deterrence
item.description: Can Seoul strengthen deterrence while remaining non-nuclear?

item.title: Why Gender Inequality Still Haunts the Economy
item.description: Why do gender wage gaps and other disparities persist despite converging education levels and mounting evidence that the public opposes such forms of inequality? The problem lies less in outdated beliefs and biases than in entrenched structural constraints to women's economic advancement.

item.title: Why North Korea Hasn’t Had Its Own ‘Reform and Opening’
item.description: Despite certain institutional similarities between China in the 1970s and North Korea today, head-to-head comparisons are deceptive.

item.title: Lesson From the May 2025 India-Pakistan Crisis: No Space for Limited War
item.description: The accelerating movement toward multi-domain non-contact warfare capabilities without any strategic restraint erodes crisis stability in South Asia.

item.title: The Affordability Crisis Is About More than Prices
item.description: Too many governments around the world are responding to affordability concerns by treating the symptoms rather than the underlying causes. Negotiating marginal price reductions with retailers might create the appearance of responsiveness, but the results will inevitably disappoint.

item.title: India Will Not Become Another China
item.description: In overlearning the lessons of China, Washington risks squandering the most important relationship it needs to counter China: India.

item.title: China’s Fallen Generals Are Getting Unexpectedly Harsh Punishments
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping is raising the floor for punishing purge targets.

item.title: A Fully Closed-off China Requires a New US Policy Response
item.description: The first airtight Chinese closure forecloses gradual reform – and American policy must be calibrated accordingly.

item.title: NATO Must Die
item.description: Some claim that the threats Europe is facing, especially after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, can create the momentum toward political union that the euro crisis and then the pandemic failed to generate. Right or wrong, one thing is clear: a functional defense union requires political union, and NATO's existence is inimical to it.

item.title: Is the United States on the Verge of Military Intervention in Cuba?
item.description: Seizing Castro could prove more costly and less effective than the capture of Maduro.

item.title: The New Narco-Sea Routes: How IUU Fishing Networks Are Fueling Drug Trafficking Across the Indo-Pacific
item.description: The same networks involved in illegal fishing are becoming logistical arteries for transnational drug trafficking. It’s a growing challenge for Indo-Pacific security.

item.title: No Limits, No Good Options
item.description: Putin’s visit to China shows that Russia is getting sucked deeper into a profoundly unequal relationship.

item.title: Taiwan’s Democratic Blind Spot: Statelessness and Legal Exclusion
item.description: Taiwan's stateless and undocumented population, numbering in the tens of thousands, has no access to basic rights, including healthcare, legal residency, and in some cases, education.

item.title: India, China, and Claims over Tawang: Views From a Field Visit to Arunachal Pradesh
item.description: In the face of the Chinese irredentist claims over Arunachal, New Delhi has attempted to transform a geopolitically sensitive frontier into a living, breathing part of the Indian nation.

item.title: A Turkish court ousts the opposition leader from his job
item.description: Deposing Ozgur Ozel is another step towards autocracy for Erdogan’s Turkey

item.title: Japanese Court Rules North Korea Owes Compensation Over Its Past Repatriation Push
item.description: Over 90,000 ethnic Koreans were enticed to leave Japan by North Korean propaganda in the early 1960s. Most remain trapped in North Korea.

item.title: Violence Looms Over Colombia’s Election
item.description: Three presidential candidates lead the field ahead of a first-round vote.

item.title: The Cultural Component of National Security
item.description: Since Russia’s full-scale invasion four years ago, Ukraine has become the world’s most consequential defense laboratory, trialing innovations that can strengthen European resilience. But the country has also demonstrated that national security depends as much on cultural identity as on military strength.

item.title: Geopolitical Chaos is Wrecking Pandemic Agreements
item.description: U.S. dysfunction is undercutting attempts at equality.

item.title: Geopolitical Chaos is Wrecking Pandemic Agreements
item.description: U.S. dysfunction is undercutting attempts at equality.

item.title: Keynes, Minsky, and the Economics of Uncertainty
item.description: Although Hyman Minsky’s version of John Maynard Keynes’s economics had a minimal impact on the mainstream during his own lifetime and in the years following, its central insights remain as relevant as ever. Both men understood that economic theory cannot get away with ignoring the fact of uncertainty.

item.title: Andhra Pradesh Is Poised to Emerge as a Strong Indian Defense Hub
item.description: Several strategic aerospace and defense projects were initiated recently in this southern Indian state.

item.title: Why Pakistan’s Economic Resilience Demands Deep Structural Reform
item.description: Depending indefinitely on remittances to conceal structural weaknesses is neither viable nor an appropriate approach from a long-term resilience perspective.

item.title: US, Philippines on Cusp of Deal on Economic Security Zone, US Official Says
item.description: Undersecretary of State Jacob Helberg's comment came days after Philippine officials shot down a U.S. proposal that the 1,260-hectare zone be placed under U.S. jurisdiction.

item.title: How the West Misreads Modi’s Success
item.description: India may be less liberal now, but it remains democratic.

item.title: How the West Misreads Modi’s Success
item.description: India may be less liberal now, but it remains democratic.

item.title: Why the Quad Was Doomed From the Start
item.description: The Philippines has replaced India in Washington’s security calculus on China.

item.title: Why the Quad Was Doomed From the Start
item.description: The Philippines has replaced India in Washington’s security calculus on China.

item.title: The Coming Contest for Asia’s Waterways
item.description: What Hormuz unleashed.

item.title: Hormuz Is a Warning for the Indo-Pacific
item.description: What Hormuz unleashed.

item.title: Kerala’s Climate Plan Speaks the Language of Justice — But Erases Caste
item.description: The vulnerability of Dalit and Adivasi communities has been historically produced through land dispossession and forced settlement on hazard-prone slopes and riverbanks.

item.title: Malaysia Puts TikTok on Notice Over ‘Offensive’ Posts About Country’s King
item.description: An account bearing the name “Sultan Ibrahim Ismail" allegedly disseminated AI-generated videos and manipulated images that defamed the monarchy.

item.title: When Internships Enable Child Labor in China
item.description: Internships are mandatory for the roughly 4 million children who graduate from secondary vocational schools each year. But some internships involve hazardous conditions – and even death.

item.title: Divide and Conquer? Ladakh’s Latest Reorganization
item.description: The creation of five new districts has observers worried that New Delhi is trying to break Buddhist-Muslim solidarity in the restive region.

item.title: Why you should (almost) always look on the bright side of life
item.description: The benefits—and dangers—of optimism

item.title: Home-schooling is taking off
item.description: In the wake of the covid-19 pandemic, more parents are teaching their children

item.title: Elon Musk is going all-in on an unproven technology
item.description: What lies behind the world’s biggest public offering

item.title: America’s economy is soaring—even with the MAGA tax
item.description: The Trump administration’s polices shows the world what not to do. But also what America keeps getting right
